# Anya — Knowledge Continuity for Special Needs Parents

> Anya preserves what a parent knows about their child with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD) — making that knowledge clear, durable, and ready for the people who will need it. Built by Vest Life, Inc., founded by Michael Pearce, an IDD attorney with 30 years of experience in Sacramento County.

Anya is not a medical tool, a therapy platform, or a general-purpose AI assistant. It is continuity infrastructure for one specific problem: most of what a parent knows about their child is never made usable for others — the routines that calm, the signs that something's wrong, the things that took years to figure out. When that parent can no longer explain it, the people who step in start from zero.

Anya solves this by helping parents capture their lived knowledge — routines, communication patterns, sensory needs, provider history, what works and what doesn't — and organizing it so it can be carried forward by caregivers, providers, family members, and future stewards.

- **Audience:** Parents of children with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD), including autism, Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, Fragile X, Prader-Willi, and complex care needs — whether the child is three or thirty-three
- **Pricing:** 7-day free trial, then $199/year (~$16.60/month). One plan, everything included.
- **Platform:** Web application
- **Privacy:** Parent-controlled. Data is never sold, shared with third parties, or used to train AI models. Entries, documents, and conversations are private by default. Parents control who gets access, what they can see, and when. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest. Built on AWS and Cloudflare infrastructure.
- **Data portability:** Everything a parent adds can be exported at any time. Wind-down plan ensures families retain access if Tenerra ceases operations.

## Pages

- [Landing Page](https://anya.tenerra.ai/): Product overview, demo conversation showing Anya in action, pricing, and founder background
- [FAQs](https://anya.tenerra.ai/faqs): 16 questions across three sections — About Anya, Understanding the Problem, and Privacy & Trust
- [About](https://anya.tenerra.ai/about): Background on Vest Life, Inc. and Michael Pearce
- [Insights](https://anya.tenerra.ai/insights): Articles on knowledge continuity, IDD planning, and the problems Anya was built to solve

## Key Concepts

- **Human Continuity Operating System (HCOS):** The framework Anya implements — ensuring what a parent knows remains clear, usable, and transferable over time without being reduced to records
- **Knowledge continuity:** The gap between what a parent knows about their child and what the people who step in after them will need to know. A Special Needs Trust is financial continuity. A guardianship is legal continuity. Anya is knowledge continuity.
- **The Vest:** The organized, durable knowledge base Anya builds with a parent over time — named for the protective layer it creates around a child's life story

## Common Questions

- **What happens to my child when I die?** Legal tools like a Special Needs Trust protect financial assets, but they cannot transfer what a parent knows — daily rhythms, communication patterns, fears, joys. Anya was built for the knowledge that no legal instrument covers.
- **How is Anya different from a binder?** A binder stores information. Anya searches across everything a parent has added, connects related details, and cites its sources when someone asks a question.
- **Is my child's information used to train AI?** No. Entries, documents, and conversations are never used to train language models or build datasets.
- **Who built Anya?** Michael Pearce — 30 years as a special needs attorney in Sacramento County — built Anya through Vest Life, Inc. because the legal tools he had were never designed to solve the knowledge continuity problem.
